argument: ONE thing to make everything better: Launch a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) of one skill, with one agent, to one real user. Why: Market validation is the highest-leverage action that unblocks everything downstream. It stops the 'build in silence' problem, validates the skill library concept, and generates real user feedback to prioritize the next 19 P0 blockers. Who: Atlas and CEO Yusif. How: Within 14 days, pick one skill (e.g., LifeSim feed-curator), deploy to production with a single agent, and invite one real user to interact. Measure: User retention and Net Promoter Score (NPS) after 7 days.
strongest_counter: The strongest counterargument is that launching an MVP with incomplete features might disappoint users and harm the brand. However, the Constitution's evidence-based approach prioritizes learning over perfection, and an MVP provides the fastest way to validate assumptions and reduce uncertainty.
evidence_cited: ECOSYSTEM-CONSTITUTION.md, VISION-FULL.md, and Session 112 CEO correction.
